steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The margin of error (2.76%) defines a symmetric interval around the sample estimate (64%). The plausible values for the true population percent are those within that interval.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Any value greater than 61.24% and less than 66.76%: 64% − 2.76% = 61.24% and 64% + 2.76% = 66.76%. Values between these bounds are plausible. ✓
  • Choice B — Any value greater than 61.24%: Ignores the upper bound — values above 66.76% are not supported by the margin of error. ✗
  • Choice C — Any value less than 61.24% or greater than 66.76%: These are exactly the values outside the confidence interval, not inside it. ✗
  • Choice D — Any value less than 66.76%: Ignores the lower bound — values below 61.24% are not supported. ✗
That is the correct answer.
